name: wormhole
description: Evidence-gated planning orchestration for AI coding agents.

interface.longDescription: Wormhole provides a local MCP planning kernel with JSONL state, evidence records, open-question gates, Markdown plan artifacts, native context packs, reversible optimization records, optimized command execution, live sub-orchestrator control, adapter-free local orchestration runs, graph-first repo indexing, graph artifacts, optional Python graph and media analysis, native media ingestion, shell hook management, discovery-driven tool generation, learned orchestration policy gates, orchestration policy baseline comparison, reasoning strategy research, printed-tool execution, native tool generation, external agent dispatch, Printing Press CLI registration, deterministic conductor scaffolds, durable brevity/minimality policy, model-profile traces, typed artifacts, and static workbench rendering. Codex should use the Wormhole loop for planning and orchestration: start a mission, start a round, query the repo index before broad grep or raw file reads, gather and record evidence, record reusable context, register tasks when parallel work is useful, use optimized command runs for large outputs, use media ingestion for PDFs/images, use shell hook planning before shell profile edits, use discovery imports/crawls before generating API tools, use learned policy only after replay-gated activation and baseline comparison, record reasoning traces when plan/critique/revision behavior matters, use local orchestration runs for deterministic task waves and outcomes, verify and run Printing Press CLIs before dispatch, generate bounded tool scaffolds when useful, request the gate, and emit the final plan only after the gate opens.
defaultPrompt: Run this through Wormhole: mission_start, round_start, repo_index_query before broad grep, record evidence, resolve questions, gate_request, then emit_plan.
Use Wormhole orchestration for this feature: record context packs, register tasks, plan local orchestration waves, and use verified external agents or printed CLIs only when needed.
Produce a cited Wormhole implementation plan and do not emit it until the evidence gate opens.
